inputs: Six synthetic code diffs of varying complexity, each handed to the model under test as the sole input.
outputs: A generated Conventional Commits message per diff, graded on the structural contract (valid type prefix, a real subject, and a body). The faithfulness/why layer is a shaped follow-up that needs a generate-then-judge runner.
decision: Which model/harness/config produces well-structured commit messages when acting as our agents -- and does a cheap or deprecated model regress on basic output discipline? Feeds routing and the broader agent-output-quality question.

config.system_prompt: You write git commit messages in the Conventional Commits style. Given a unified diff, produce exactly one commit message: a subject line of the form `type(scope): summary` (type is one of feat, fix, docs, refactor, perf, test, build, ci, chore; scope optional), then a blank line, then a body of one or more sentences explaining what changed and WHY. Respond with ONLY the commit message text -- no code fences, no preamble, no quotes, no trailing commentary.

tasks.prompt:
Write a Conventional Commits message for this diff:

```diff
--- a/src/cli.rs
+++ b/src/cli.rs
@@ struct RunArgs {
     pub spec: PathBuf,
+    /// Fail instead of warn when the sample cannot resolve the declared effect.
+    #[arg(long)]
+    pub strict_power: bool,
 }
```

Write a Conventional Commits message for this diff:

```diff
--- a/src/config.rs
+++ b/src/config.rs
@@ pub struct Config {
     pub port: u16,
+    /// Serve without the bearer gate when behind a trusted network layer.
+    #[serde(default)]
+    pub trust_network: bool,
 }
--- a/README.md
+++ b/README.md
@@
+- `trust_network`: skip the auth gate behind a private tailnet or proxy.
```

Write a Conventional Commits message for this diff:

```diff
--- a/src/lib.rs
+++ b/src/lib.rs
@@
-// Legacy path kept during the v1->v2 migration; no caller since #114.
-pub fn legacy_score(trials: &[Trial]) -> f64 {
-    trials.iter().filter(|t| t.passed).count() as f64 / trials.len() as f64
-}
-
 pub fn score(trials: &[Trial]) -> Estimate {
```
